/solutions/ai-safety.rationale: AI safety was about securing AI workloads; ai-pentesting is the direct successor covering AI-specific security testing.

/solutions/backup-recovery.rationale: Backup-recovery covered backup and restore workflows; rapid-recovery is the closest live heir covering the same recovery-from-backup topic.

/solutions/data-security.rationale: Data-security was a general data-protection page; encryption is the most focused live heir covering at-rest and in-transit data protection.

/solutions/development-environments.rationale: Development-environments was about spinning up isolated dev envs; environment-cloning is the direct live replacement.

/solutions/devops.rationale: Broad DevOps category page removed; no single live solution covers the same wide DevOps scope without forcing a poor fit.

/solutions/disaster-recovery.rationale: Disaster-recovery maps cleanly to rapid-recovery, the live solution dedicated to restoring services after outages.

/solutions/enterprise.rationale: Generic enterprise landing page removed; no live solution covers a comparable broad enterprise-features scope.

/solutions/enterprise-scaling.rationale: Enterprise-scaling page removed; no live heir addresses horizontal scaling or capacity planning at that level of specificity.

/solutions/infrastructure-protection.rationale: Infrastructure-protection focused on hardening infrastructure against threats; vulnerability-management is the closest live heir covering infrastructure-level security gaps.

/solutions/preemptive-defense.rationale: Preemptive-defense was about proactive security posture; continuous-security-testing is its direct renamed successor.

/solutions/system-portability.rationale: System-portability was about avoiding platform lock-in; vendor-lock-in is the live page covering the same topic.

/solutions/threat-response.rationale: Threat-response covered rapid reaction to active incidents; rapid-recovery is the live heir focused on fast service restoration.

/transparency.rationale: No /transparency page in the manifest; transparency content was removed with no discoverable heir.

/yr.rationale: Unknown legacy path with no discoverable heir.

patterns.rationale: Concatenated legal paths caused by old relative-href bug in legal pages. Second segment is the link target the user intended to reach (they were on the first-segment page and clicked a relative link expecting to land on the second-segment page). Pattern covers all 30 theoretical combinations; 11 observed in GSC. NOTE: /acceptable-use-policy is absent from route-manifest.json — any concat path whose second segment is acceptable-use-policy (e.g. /terms-of-service/acceptable-use-policy, observed in GSC) will redirect to a potentially missing page. Verify that /acceptable-use-policy is live before deploying, or add it to the manifest.
Mangled "contact" URLs Google crawled from broken relative links (9 observed: /arntact, /dentact, /esntact, /frntact, /jantact, /runtact, /trntact, /zhntact).
Legacy console/login paths; dashboard now at /account (served by Worker). Covers /console, /console/login, and query-string register variants.
Multi-page cheat-sheet was consolidated into a single Marp-rendered page (8 subpaths observed).
